what: Classify genes by multi-round monotonic time-course patterns — classify_timecourse_by_round
title: Classify genes by multi-round monotonic time-course patterns — classify_timecourse_by_round
text: function classifies genes based monotonic temporal trends using iterative Spearman correlation strategy across progressively trimmed time windows. round, genes exhibiting significant positive negative monotonicity assigned groups corresponding round trend first becomes detectable.

title: Classify genes by multi-round monotonic time-course patterns — classify_timecourse_by_round
code: classify_timecourse_by_round(   mat,   rho_cut = 0.75,   min_len = 4,   trim_side = c("best", "head", "tail") )

title: Classify genes by multi-round monotonic time-course patterns — classify_timecourse_by_round
text: mat numeric matrix genes rows time-ordered samples columns. Row names must correspond gene identifiers. rho_cut Numeric scalar specifying absolute Spearman correlation threshold calling monotonic trend. Default 0.75. min_len Integer specifying minimum number samples required perform trend classification. Default 4. trim_side Character string specifying trim time window iteration. One :

title: Classify genes by multi-round monotonic time-course patterns — classify_timecourse_by_round
text: data.frame one row per gene following columns:gene,gene_group,type

title: Classify genes by multi-round monotonic time-course patterns — classify_timecourse_by_round
text: time window shortened removing one boundary sample (head, tail, automatically selected) round, fewer min_len samples remain.

title: Calculate gene-wise correlation to target gene profile — correlate_genes_to_target

title: Calculate gene-wise correlation to target gene profile — correlate_genes_to_target
Calculate gene-wise correlation to target gene profile — correlate_genes_to_target

title: Calculate gene-wise correlation to target gene profile — correlate_genes_to_target
text: data.frame one row per gene correlation values. returned columns include: \describe   \itemfeature_idRow names assay matrix.   \itemgeneGene label. Uses \coderowData(se)$gene available,   otherwise row names.   \itemcorrelationCorrelation gene target profile.

what: Extract positive and negative contributors for a given PCA component — get_pc_contributors
title: Extract positive and negative contributors for a given PCA component — get_pc_contributors
text: function extracts top contributing features (e.g. proteins/genes) specified principal component (PC) FactoMineR::PCA result. supports signed loadings (coordinates) direction-aware interpretation contribution values importance ranking, can optionally merge feature annotations SummarizedExperiment object.

title: Extract positive and negative contributors for a given PCA component — get_pc_contributors
code: get_pc_contributors(   pca.res,   se_obj = NULL,   pc = 1,   n = 20,   use = c("coord", "contrib") )

title: Extract positive and negative contributors for a given PCA component — get_pc_contributors
text: pca.res PCA object returned FactoMineR::PCA. se_obj SummarizedExperiment object. provided, row annotations (rowData(se_obj)) joined PCA variables using Protein.Ids. Default NULL. pc Integer. principal component extract (e.g. 1 PC1). Default 1. n Integer. Number top features return direction (positive negative). Default 20. use Character. PCA metric use: "coord" Use signed coordinates (loadings). preserves     directionality suitable identifying positive vs negative     contributors along PC. "contrib" Use contribution values. ranks features     importance PC encode direction. Default "coord".

title: Extract positive and negative contributors for a given PCA component — get_pc_contributors
text: list following components: PC Character string indicating selected PC (e.g. "Dim.1"). metric metric used: "coord" "contrib". positive data frame top n positively contributing       features ("coord"). negative data frame top n negatively contributing       features ("coord").

title: Extract positive and negative contributors for a given PCA component — get_pc_contributors
text: function operates pca.res$var[[use]], : coord represents signed loadings (projection feature     onto PC axis), allowing interpretation positive vs negative directions. contrib represents percentage contribution feature     variance PC, without directional information. se_obj provided, feature-level annotations merged rowData(se_obj) Protein.Ids. Gene symbols extracted Genes column (taking first symbol semicolon). multiple protein entries map gene, consider collapsing results gene level downstream.

title: Extract positive and negative contributors for a given PCA component — get_pc_contributors
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(FactoMineR)  # Run PCA pca.res <- PCA(t(expr_matrix), scale.unit = TRUE, graph = FALSE)  # Extract top contributors on PC1 using signed coordinates res_pc1 <- get_pc_contributors(pca.res, pc = 1, n = 30, use = "coord")  res_pc1$positive res_pc1$negative  # Using contribution values instead of signed loadings res_pc1_contrib <- get_pc_contributors(pca.res, pc = 1, n = 30, use = "contrib") } # }

what: Interactive or static volcano plot — ivolcano
title: Interactive or static volcano plot — ivolcano
text: function creates volcano plot optional interactivity via ggiraph. Code https://github.com/YuLab-SMU/ivolcano

title: Interactive or static volcano plot — ivolcano
code: ivolcano(   data,   logFC_col = "logFC",   pval_col = "adj.P.Val",   gene_col = "gene",   title = "",   interactive = TRUE,   onclick_fun = NULL,   pval_cutoff = 0.05,   logFC_cutoff = 1,   top_n = 10,   label_mode = "separate",   fontface = "italic",   label_sig_only = TRUE,   threshold_line = list(color = "black", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 0.5),   sig_colors = c(Up = "red", Down = "blue", Not_Significant = "grey70"),   size_by = "none" )

title: Interactive or static volcano plot — ivolcano
text: data Input data frame. logFC_col Column name storing log fold change. pval_col Column storing adjusted P value. gene_col Column storing gene names. title Plot title. interactive Whether use ggiraph. onclick_fun Optional function onclick events. pval_cutoff FDR threshold. logFC_cutoff LogFC threshold. top_n Number labels draw. label_mode Label mode. fontface Label font face. label_sig_only Whether label significant genes. threshold_line Line settings cutoff. sig_colors Colors //Not_Significant. size_by Optional scaling variable.

title: Interactive or static volcano plot — ivolcano
text: list static ggplot object interactive girafe object.

what: Visualize DEG dynamic trends by Mfuzz cluster — plot_deg_trends
title: Visualize DEG dynamic trends by Mfuzz cluster — plot_deg_trends
text: Visualizes dynamic expression trends differentially expressed genes (DEGs) grouped Mfuzz clustering results stored `rowData(se)`. function summarizes gene expression across conditions, optionally scales expression per gene (z-score), displays cluster-level average trends optional single-gene trajectories.

title: Visualize DEG dynamic trends by Mfuzz cluster — plot_deg_trends
code: plot_deg_trends(   se,   assay_name = "conc",   group_by = "condition",   scale_by_gene = TRUE,   show_all_genes = TRUE,   show_ci = FALSE,   ci_level = 0.95 )

title: Visualize DEG dynamic trends by Mfuzz cluster — plot_deg_trends
text: se SummarizedExperiment object. Must contain: assay specified assay_name column colData(se) specified group_by Columns gene, gene_group, type rowData(se) assay_name Character. Name assay visualize. Default "conc". group_by Character. Column name colData(se) used define condition time ordering. Default "condition". scale_by_gene Logical. TRUE, expression values scaled per gene (z-score across conditions). Recommended dynamic pattern visualization. Default TRUE. show_all_genes Logical. TRUE, individual gene trajectories shown semi-transparent grey lines. Default TRUE. show_ci Logical. TRUE, add 95% confidence intervals cluster-level mean trend condition. Default FALSE. ci_level Numeric. Confidence level used show_ci = TRUE. Default 0.95.

title: Visualize DEG dynamic trends by Mfuzz cluster — plot_deg_trends
text: ggplot2 object showing dynamic expression trends DEG cluster.

title: Visualize DEG dynamic trends by Mfuzz cluster — plot_deg_trends
text: function performs following steps: Filters genes labeled type == "DEGs" rowData(se). Computes mean expression per gene per condition. Optionally standardizes expression per gene. Computes cluster-level mean trends optional confidence intervals. Generates faceted line plot gene_group. function assumes Mfuzz clustering results already computed stored rowData(se)$gene_group.

title: Visualize DEG dynamic trends by Mfuzz cluster — plot_deg_trends

what: Plot multi-round logFC trends for monotonic gene groups — plot_FC_trend
title: Plot multi-round logFC trends for monotonic gene groups — plot_FC_trend
text: function visualizes log fold-change (logFC) trajectories genes classified multi-round monotonic time-course patterns (e.g. Up1, Down2). Individual gene trajectories shown semi-transparent lines, group-level mean trajectory overlaid pattern.

title: Plot multi-round logFC trends for monotonic gene groups — plot_FC_trend
Plot multi-round logFC trends for monotonic gene groups — plot_FC_trend
text: se SummarizedExperiment object containing concentration-based expression data log fold-change values. group_by Character string specifying column colData(se) used define experimental conditions. Default "condition".

title: Plot multi-round logFC trends for monotonic gene groups — plot_FC_trend
text: ggplot object showing logFC trajectories   faceted gene trend group.

title: Plot multi-round logFC trends for monotonic gene groups — plot_FC_trend
text: Genes grouped according assigned trend group, facets ordered round index, * * displayed paired groups within round. function performs following steps: Convert SummarizedExperiment object gene-level         data frame using se2conc(). Retain genes classified . Reshape logFC values long format plotting. Reorder gene groups paired rounds         (Up1, Down1, Up2, Down2, ...). Plot individual gene trajectories overlay group-level         mean trends. Facet labels include number genes (n) group.

title: Plot multi-round logFC trends for monotonic gene groups — plot_FC_trend

what: Plot heatmap with row-wise summary line using ComplexHeatmap — plot_heatmap_withline
title: Plot heatmap with row-wise summary line using ComplexHeatmap — plot_heatmap_withline
text: function draws heatmap using ComplexHeatmap adds custom row-side panel summarizes row cluster (defined row_split) line plot (mean median across columns column groups). particularly useful visualizing module-level trends cluster-level expression profiles omics data.

title: Plot heatmap with row-wise summary line using ComplexHeatmap — plot_heatmap_withline
code: plot_heatmap_withline(   mat = NULL,   row_split = NULL,   column_split = NULL,   top_annotation = NULL,   show_column_names = FALSE,   ht.col.list = list(col_range = c(-2, 0, 2), col_color = c("#08519C", "white",     "#A50F15")),   border = TRUE,   line.size = 0.1,   line.col = "grey90",   mline.size = 2,   mline.col = "#CC3333",   set.md = "mean",   textbox.pos = c(0.5, 0.8),   textbox.size = 8,   panel.arg = c(2, 0.25, 4, "grey90", NA),   ... )

title: Plot heatmap with row-wise summary line using ComplexHeatmap — plot_heatmap_withline
text: mat numeric matrix. Rows typically represent genes/features columns represent samples/conditions. matrix internally coerced matrix needed. row_split vector factor length nrow(mat) used split rows clusters groups. unique level defines one row group. column_split vector factor length ncol(mat) used split columns groups. provided, summary lines calculated group level; otherwise, summaries calculated across columns. top_annotation HeatmapAnnotation object (ComplexHeatmap) added top heatmap. Default NULL. show_column_names Logical. Whether display column names heatmap. Default FALSE. ht.col.list list controlling color mapping heatmap. col_range Numeric vector length 3 specifying breakpoints color scale. col_color Character vector colors corresponding col_range. border Logical. Whether draw borders around heatmap cells. Passed ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap. Default TRUE. line.size Numeric. Line width grid lines heatmap body. Default 0.1. line.col Character. Color grid lines heatmap body. Default "grey90". mline.size Numeric. Line width summary line drawn right annotation panel. Default 2. mline.col Character. Color summary line drawn right annotation panel. Default "#CC3333". set.md Character. Method summarizing values within cluster. Either "mean" "median". Default "mean". textbox.pos Numeric vector length 2 giving relative position (NPC units) annotation text box inside panel. Default c(0.5, 0.8). textbox.size Numeric. Font size annotation text shown panel. Default 8. panel.arg vector length 5 controlling layout appearance right-side summary panel created anno_link: [1] Height panel (cm). [2] Gap heatmap panel (cm). [3] Width panel (cm). [4] Fill color panel background. [5] Border color panel. Use NA remove border. Default c(2, 0.25, 4, "grey90", NA). ... Additional arguments passed ComplexHeatmap::Heatmap.

title: Plot heatmap with row-wise summary line using ComplexHeatmap — plot_heatmap_withline
text: Heatmap object ComplexHeatmap. object can   modified, combined heatmaps, drawn using draw().

title: Plot heatmap with row-wise summary line using ComplexHeatmap — plot_heatmap_withline
text: function performs following steps: Split rows according row_split. Assign random colors row group display block   annotations right side. row group, compute either mean median expression   across columns (across column groups column_split provided). Plot summarized values line within custom panel aligned   corresponding row cluster. visualization particularly suitable showing module-level cluster-level trends, temporal patterns condition-specific shifts expression.

title: Plot heatmap with row-wise summary line using ComplexHeatmap — plot_heatmap_withline
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(ComplexHeatmap)  # Example matrix mat <- matrix(rnorm(200), nrow = 20) rownames(mat) <- paste0("Gene", 1:20) colnames(mat) <- paste0("Sample", 1:10)  # Row and column grouping row_grp <- rep(LETTERS[1:4], each = 5) col_grp <- rep(c("Ctrl", "Treat"), each = 5)  # Draw heatmap with summary lines ht <- plot_heatmap_withline(   mat = mat,   row_split = row_grp,   column_split = col_grp,   set.md = "mean" )  draw(ht) } # }

what: Construct a SummarizedExperiment object from raw expression table — rawdata2se
title: Construct a SummarizedExperiment object from raw expression table — rawdata2se
text: function reads raw expression table, performs feature-level filtering, optional outlier masking, missing-value imputation, stability filtering, returns SummarizedExperiment object together diagnostic gene lists.

title: Construct a SummarizedExperiment object from raw expression table — rawdata2se
code: rawdata2se(   exp_file,   obs_col = "Genes",   raw_prefix = "auto",   enable_detect_outlier_gene = FALSE,   fc_threshold = 5,   min_valid_groups = 0,   frac_NA_threshold = 0.5,   min_stable_groups = 0,   cv_threshold = 0.5 )

title: Construct a SummarizedExperiment object from raw expression table — rawdata2se
text: exp_file Path raw expression table file. file must contain one column specifying feature identifiers multiple columns containing raw expression values. obs_col Name column exp_file used define feature identifiers. substring first semicolon (;) used feature name. raw_prefix Character string used identify raw expression columns. \'auto\' (default), raw intensity columns detected automatically common raw file suffixes \'raw\', \'mzML\', \'mzXML\', similar filename-style endings. enable_detect_outlier_gene Logical flag indicating whether mask extreme outlier measurements within condition based fold-change median. fc_threshold Numeric threshold fold-change–based outlier detection. Values exceeding fold change relative condition median set NA outlier detection enabled. min_valid_groups Minimum number conditions feature must pass missing-value filter retained. Features fewer valid conditions removed. frac_NA_threshold Maximum allowed fraction missing values within condition condition considered valid given feature. min_stable_groups Minimum number conditions feature must show low variability (coefficient variation cv_threshold) retained. cv_threshold Numeric threshold coefficient variation (CV) used define feature stability within condition.

title: Construct a SummarizedExperiment object from raw expression table — rawdata2se
text: list class "RawDataSE" containing: se: SummarizedExperiment object storing raw       intensities, imputed intensities, CPM-normalized values,       z-scored expression. un_stable_gene: data frame features removed due       high variability across conditions. missing_gene_df: data frame features removed due       excessive missing values.

what: Run GSEA (Gene Set Enrichment Analysis) — run_gsea
title: Run GSEA (Gene Set Enrichment Analysis) — run_gsea
text: Performs GO KEGG GSEA using ranked gene statistics. function dynamically loads clusterProfiler annotation packages needed, making optional dependencies.

title: Run GSEA (Gene Set Enrichment Analysis) — run_gsea
code: run_gsea(   df,   gene_col = "gene",   stat_col = "log2FC",   db = c("GO", "KEGG"),   ont = "ALL",   species = c("human", "mouse"),   keyType = "SYMBOL",   qvalue_cutoff = 0.05,   minGSSize = 10,   maxGSSize = 5000,   pAdjustMethod = "BH",   eps = 1e-10,   use_internal_data = FALSE,   seed = 123 )

title: Run GSEA (Gene Set Enrichment Analysis) — run_gsea
text: df data frame containing ranking statistics gene IDs. gene_col Column storing gene IDs. stat_col Column storing ranking values (e.g. log2FC). db Database GSEA: "GO" "KEGG". ont GO ontology ("BP","CC","MF",""). species Species: "human" "mouse". keyType Gene ID type (default SYMBOL). qvalue_cutoff Q-value filter. minGSSize Minimum gene set size. maxGSSize Maximum gene set size. pAdjustMethod Multiple testing correction. eps GSEA numeric convergence threshold. use_internal_data whether using internal KEGG data seed Random seed.

title: Run GSEA (Gene Set Enrichment Analysis) — run_gsea
text: gseaResult object.

what: Perform fuzzy clustering on gene-level time-course matrix using Mfuzz — run_mfuzz_clustering
title: Perform fuzzy clustering on gene-level time-course matrix using Mfuzz — run_mfuzz_clustering
text: Applies fuzzy c-means clustering (via Mfuzz) gene--sample numeric matrix (e.g., log2FC, expression, kinetic values). gene assigned cluster highest membership score. function typically used classify dynamic genes (e.g., DEGs) temporal response patterns.

title: Perform fuzzy clustering on gene-level time-course matrix using Mfuzz — run_mfuzz_clustering
Perform fuzzy clustering on gene-level time-course matrix using Mfuzz — run_mfuzz_clustering
text: mat numeric matrix data.frame genes rows samples/time points columns. Row names must contain gene IDs. k Integer. Number clusters (default = 10). min_sd Numeric. Minimum standard deviation threshold filtering low-variance genes prior clustering (default = 0).

title: Perform fuzzy clustering on gene-level time-course matrix using Mfuzz — run_mfuzz_clustering
text: tibble three columns: gene Gene identifier (rownames input matrix). gene_group Cluster assignment label (e.g., "C1", "C2", ...). type Character label, default set "DEGs".

title: Perform fuzzy clustering on gene-level time-course matrix using Mfuzz — run_mfuzz_clustering
text: workflow includes: Filtering genes row-wise standard deviation. Z-score standardization using Mfuzz::standardise(). Automatic estimation fuzzifier parameter m. Fuzzy c-means clustering. Hard assignment based maximum membership.

title: Perform fuzzy clustering on gene-level time-course matrix using Mfuzz — run_mfuzz_clustering
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Example: clustering a log2FC matrix set.seed(1) mat <- matrix(rnorm(1000), nrow = 100) rownames(mat) <- paste0("Gene", 1:100) colnames(mat) <- paste0("T", 1:10)  res <- run_mfuzz_clustering(mat, k = 5) head(res) } # }

what: Save plots in multiple formats with standardized resolution — saveplot
title: Save plots in multiple formats with standardized resolution — saveplot
text: Save plot object PDF, TIFF, PNG formats simultaneously predefined scaling rules width, height, resolution. Output files automatically organized subdirectories Fig/ folder.

title: Save plots in multiple formats with standardized resolution — saveplot
code: saveplot(object, filenames = NULL, width = NULL, height = NULL, dpi = 600)

title: Save plots in multiple formats with standardized resolution — saveplot
text: object plot object saved. Can ggplot object grid graphical object gList. filenames character string specifying base filename (without file extension) output figures. width Numeric. Figure width inches. Must provided. height Numeric. Figure height inches. Must provided. dpi Numeric. Resolution dots per inch. Default 600.

title: Save plots in multiple formats with standardized resolution — saveplot
text: Invisibly returns NULL. function called side effect   writing figure files disk.

title: Save plots in multiple formats with standardized resolution — saveplot
text: function designed publication-quality figure export, supporting ggplot objects grid graphical objects (e.g., gList). function creates directory structure: Fig/PDF PDF output Fig/TIFF TIFF output Fig/PNG PNG output File dimensions scaled internally: PDF: width height multiplied 2. TIFF: width height multiplied 1000. PNG: width height multiplied 500. PNG output, dpi > 600, resolution automatically set 300; otherwise, dpi/2 used. object class gList, plot rendered using grid.draw(); otherwise, print() used.

title: Save plots in multiple formats with standardized resolution — saveplot
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(ggplot2) p <- ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) + geom_point()  saveplot(   object = p,   filenames = "mtcars_scatter",   width = 6,   height = 4,   dpi = 600 ) } # }
